sub_questions: What is the classification of Algarve roads (N125, N124, N2, A22, municipal, dirt tracks) as barriers vs corridors for lost dogs?
What traffic volume data exists by road segment and time of day for the Algarve?
Which intersections/road segments have highest animal accident rates (indicating crossing corridors)?
Is the A22 motorway an absolute barrier? Do dogs get onto it and what happens?
Are there known underpasses or culverts animals use to cross major Algarve roads?
What is the quality of OpenStreetMap coverage for Algarve rural roads?
What types of water sources exist across the Algarve (rivers, reservoirs, irrigation channels, pools, troughs) and what is their distribution?
Which water sources are seasonal vs permanent (especially July-September drying)?
What GIS datasets exist for Algarve hydrography (SNIRH, DGT, OSM water layer)?
What is the density and distribution of irrigation infrastructure across Litoral, Barrocal, and Serra zones?
Are there known wildlife drinking points that dogs might also use?
What are the main vegetation/terrain zones (Litoral, Barrocal, Serra, coastal agricultural) and their implications for a hiding or moving dog?
Which vegetation types are impenetrable vs permeable to a medium/large dog?
Where are the largest continuous uninhabited areas (dog surviving 7+ days)?
Are there known wildlife corridors (fox, wild boar) dogs might also use?
Where are fire risk zones and recent burn areas (and their movement/survival implications)?
What elevation data is relevant to movement modeling (Serra de Monchique 902m)?
What GIS datasets exist (ICNF, Corine Land Cover, PDM plans)?
What is the map of agricultural zones by type (livestock farms, intensive horticulture, traditional extensive)?
Where are registered hunting zones (zonas de caça) in the Algarve?
What is tourist density by zone and season?
Are there known animal feeding points attracting survival-mode dogs?
Do dogs move toward urban centers when hungry?
What are the main hazard types for lost dogs in the Algarve (traffic, abandoned wells/poços, pesticides, traps, wild boar, cliffs, heat) and their spatial distribution?
How common are abandoned wells (poços) in the Algarve and is there data on their distribution?
Where are wild boar zones (Serra de Monchique, Caldeirão)?
Where are coastal cliffs/drops (Sagres, Aljezur)?
Where are extreme heat zones (south-facing slopes, urban heat islands)?
Do official hazard maps exist from GNR, ICNF, or municipal civil protection?
Are there documented cases of dogs found in dangerous situations (wells, trapped) in the Algarve?
required_formats: per sub-topic: (1) available data sources, (2) data quality assessment, (3) key geographic patterns, (4) encoding approach, (5) local knowledge gaps
required_section_headings: ## 1. Road Network — Attractors, Barriers, and Transport Vectors
## 3. Vegetation and Terrain Zones — Cover, Concealment, and Barriers
## 4. Human Activity Zones — Food, Contact, and Hazard
scope_conditions: geographic scope: Algarve region, Portugal only
application: lost dog rescue OS — recommendations must be actionable for search teams
each section requires 5 named sub-fields: data sources, quality, geographic patterns, encoding approach, local knowledge gaps
